title: BIDI text painting skipped tests.
body:
As part of the latest [skip test audit](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/86396), there are still several tests that are skipped in `text_painter_rtl_test.dart` that don't have a bug to track them. We can use this as a tracking bug for getting them turned back on or marked as intentional if that is the just behaviour we want.

The tests in question are:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/40739568ab80988d8f2671bf6ec43539ce983771/packages/flutter/test/painting/text_painter_rtl_test.dart#L37

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/40739568ab80988d8f2671bf6ec43539ce983771/packages/flutter/test/painting/text_painter_rtl_test.dart#L169

title: Document "ghost run" and its interaction with `Paragraph.getBoxesForRange`
body:
Current the only documentation I could find is a code comment: https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/cc9f859004f88a84f6d42e234c8be0ecaf66c46f/third_party/txt/src/txt/paragraph_txt.cc#L742-L752

>       // A "ghost" run is a run that does not impact the layout, breaking,
>      // alignment, width, etc but is still "visible" through getRectsForRange.
>      // For example, trailing whitespace on centered text can be scrolled
>      // through with the caret but will not wrap the line.
>      //
>      // Here, we add an additional run for the whitespace, but dont
>      // let it impact metrics. After layout of the whitespace run, we do not
>      // add its width into the x-offset adjustment, effectively nullifying its
>     // impact on the layout.

which implies `getRectsForRange` can return text boxes that are outside of the paragraph's bounding box if called on a ghost run. It's likely a common assumption that text boxes is always within the paragraph's bounding box so that should be documented somewhere.

title: [MacOS] Trailing whitespace in multiline TextField overflows and doesn't go to newline.
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Use a multiline TextField.
2. Input a lot of whitespace in the content tail.
3. Whitespace is overflow and not switch to a newline (Tap Ctrl + A to select all).

**Expected results:** Auto switch to a new line.

**Actual results:** Whitespace is overflow.

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
const SizedBox(
    width: 400,
    height: 200,
    child: TextField(
      maxLines: null,
      keyboardType: TextInputType.multiline,
      decoration: InputDecoration(
        border: InputBorder.none,
        filled: true,
        fillColor: Colors.pink
      ),
      expands: true,
    ),
  )
```

</details>

<details>
  <summary>Logs</summary>
<!-- Finally, paste the output of running `flutter doctor -v` here. -->

```
[✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 2.8.1, on macOS 12.0.1 21A559 darwin-arm, locale
    zh-Hans-CN)
    • Flutter version 2.8.1 at /Volumes/AOSP/flutter
    • Upstream repository https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git
    • Framework revision 77d935af4d (2 months ago), 2021-12-16 08:37:33 -0800
    • Engine revision 890a5fca2e
    • Dart version 2.15.1

[✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 32.0.0)
    • Android SDK at /Users/jinguoyang/Library/Android/sdk
    • Platform android-32, build-tools 32.0.0
    • Java binary at: /Applications/Android
      Studio.app/Contents/jre/Contents/Home/bin/java
```

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15059084/155669905-5de481ac-716f-421e-987b-f1c705a036bc.mov
comment_summary: Comments confirm this is distinct from mobile behavior: on macOS/desktop, trailing whitespace in a multiline TextField does not trigger line wrap, unlike Sublime Text or other native editors. On iOS/mobile, whitespace also does not wrap, but subsequent characters start on a new line. Selection extending horizontally outside the field when selecting text with trailing whitespace is noted as a separate bug. Attributed to Skia-level text layout behavior. No fix; stalled.

title: canvas.drawLine() does not paint correctly on Samsung Galaxy Note 20 Ultra
body:
## Steps to Reproduce 1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample using Samsung Galaxy S4 (GT-I9500) **Expected results:** [Dartpad code](https://dartpad.dev/?id=a02554870741d78d26f0669b808ae5ab) **Actual results:**
Code sample ``` import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() { runApp(MyApp()); } class MyApp extends StatelessWidget { const MyApp({Key key}) : super(key: key); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return MaterialApp(debugShowCheckedModeBanner: false, home: HomeScreen(), ); } } class HomeScreen extends StatelessWidget { const HomeScreen({Key key}) : super(key: key); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return Scaffold( appBar: AppBar(title: Text('UnderlineInputBorder'),centerTitle: true,), body: Container( margin: EdgeInsets.all(20), child: TextField( decoration: InputDecoration(enabledBorder: UnderlineInputBorder()), ), ), ); } } ```
Flutter Doctor ``` [√] Flutter (Channel stable, 3.0.5, on Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.19044.1889], locale en-US) • Flutter version 3.0.5 at C:\src\flutter • Upstream repository https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git • Framework revision f1875d570e (7 weeks ago), 2022-07-13 11:24:16 -0700 • Engine revision e85ea0e79c • Dart version 2.17.6 • DevTools version 2.12.2 [√] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 33.0.0) • Android SDK at C:\Users\****\AppData\Local\Android\Sdk • Platform android-33, build-tools 33.0.0 • Java binary at: C:\Program Files\Android\Android Studio1\jre\bin\java • Java version OpenJDK Runtime Environment (build 11.0.13+0-b1751.21-8125866) • All Android licenses accepted. [√] Chrome - develop for the web • Chrome at C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe [!] Visual Studio - develop for Windows (Visual Studio Community 2022 17.2.6) • Visual Studio at C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community • Visual Studio Community 2022 version 17.2.32630.192 X Visual Studio is missing necessary components. Please re-run the Visual Studio installer for the "Desktop development with C++" workload, and include these components: MSVC v142 - VS 2019 C++ x64/x86 build tools - If there are multiple build tool versions available, install the latest C++ CMake tools for Windows Windows 10 SDK [√] Android Studio (version 2021.3) • Android Studio at C:\Program Files\Android\Android Studio1 • Flutter plugin can be installed from: https://plugins.jetbrains.com/plugin/9212-flutter • Dart plugin can be installed from: https://plugins.jetbrains.com/plugin/6351-dart • Java version OpenJDK Runtime Environment (build 11.0.13+0-b1751.21-8125866) [√] VS Code (version 1.70.2) • VS Code at C:\Users\*****\AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code • Flutter extension can be installed from: https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=Dart-Code.flutter [√] Connected device (5 available) • GT I9500 (mobile) • 4d009104ce6550bf • android-arm • Android 5.0.1 (API 21) • SM A536E (mobile) • adb-R5CT21L8F4N-l1CDkM._adb-tls-connect._tcp • android-arm64 • Android 12 (API 31) • Windows (desktop) • windows • windows-x64 • Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.19044.1889] • Chrome (web) • chrome • web-javascript • Google Chrome 104.0.5112.102 • Edge (web) • edge • web-javascript • Microsoft Edge 104.0.1293.70 [√] HTTP Host Availability • All required HTTP hosts are available ! Doctor found issues in 1 category. ```
Other devices are painting as expected, however I got painting issue on this device (GT-I9500). A year ago I faced similar painting issue discussed here: [BoxDecoration has bug when using both Border and BorderRadius](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/86794) I tried to change **UnderlineInputBorder** to **OutlineInputBorder** and got expected results without any issues, because OutlineInputBorder uses `canvas.drawPath()` to draw it. On the other side, UnderlineInputBorder uses `canvas.drawLine() ` which is causing this issue. If I am not wrong **drawLine** calls native code called **Canvas_drawLine**
comment_summary: Issue not reproduced on Samsung Tab A7 (Android 11/12) by triagers, but reporter confirms it persists on GT-I9500 (Android 5.0.1) through version 3.3.0. A related issue (#145872) on a different Samsung device was closed in favor of this one, so the issue remains open; the GT-I9500 is discontinued but the rendering defect via drawLine on older Samsung hardware is kept under investigation.

title: Provide an API to detect if a TextPosition is located at a soft word wrap
body:
Currently it is not possible to detect if a TextPosition is located at a soft word wrap. We should provide an API, most likely through `RenderEditable` that gives this information. This is necessary to accomplish the use case below.

## Use case
On iOS we want to be able to toggle the toolbar if we tap on the same `TextPosition` regardless of the `TextAffinity` when our tap is not at a soft word wrap. When our tap is at a soft word wrap we want to hide the toolbar if the `TextAffinity` has changed and toggle the toolbar if it hasn't.

## Proposal
Provide an API through `RenderEditable` that tells us if a given `TextPosition` is located at a word wrap.

title: Extending to paragraph/or word boundary on MacOS should default to the `downstream` position when at a word wrap
body:
When using keyboard shortcuts on MacOS
* shift + option + up/down to extend to the next/previous paragraph boundary
* shift + option + left/right to extend to the next/previous word boundary

And the caret is positioned at a word wrap initially, and the selection is extended, when returning to the original position (collapsing at the base when the selection inverts) the resulting selection should to default to the `downstream`  position.

Current Behavior:
The selection inherits the `affinity` from `selection.base.affinity`.

Expected Behavior:
The selection defaults to a `downstream` affinity.

Expected|Actual
---|---
<video src="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/948037/215957886-fbafa1e7-8b3a-4df4-8403-f88dc84e372d.mov" />|<video src="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/948037/215958318-7da3618b-2362-4791-97f9-7c3c19e97b33.mov" />
comment_summary: Comments point to PR #116549 discussion for context.

title: [Windows] Incorrect character deletion in right-to-left texts
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

Type rtl text to multiline textfield :
(DO NOT COPY AND PASTE)

بابا آب داد
با مهربانی
4طتانا جمعا 4واتا +یکسوییت 20متری
بنبابا 40ساله

### Expected results

The letter to the right of the cursor should be deleted.
(It should be noted that when I copy the text from above and paste it in the text field, everything works correctly.)

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/b3d09305-3b20-4388-b938-2ce451217a79



### Actual results

But when I type the rtl text , the letter on the left side of the cursor is deleted.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/31f9ee2d-3aeb-441a-8568-97dcf7fdefe7



### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter_localizations/flutter_localizations.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      locale: const Locale("ar"),
      localizationsDelegates: const [
        GlobalMaterialLocalizations.delegate,
        GlobalWidgetsLocalizations.delegate,
        GlobalCupertinoLocalizations.delegate,
      ],
      supportedLocales: const [Locale("ar")],
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({Key? key, required this.title}) : super(key: key);

  final String title;

  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: const Center(
        child: Padding(
          padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
          child: TextField(
            maxLines: 1000,
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

<details>
<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/d06fc103-aca5-4df4-8242-fdc400247346

</details>


### Logs

<details><summary>Logs</summary>

```console
[Paste your logs here]
```

</details>


### Flutter Doctor output

<details><summary>Doctor output</summary>


comment_summary: Comments confirmed reproduction on Windows with RTL (Persian/Farsi) multiline text that was manually typed (not pasted); pasting the same text works correctly. The root cause was identified as a caret placement issue: when text contains CRLF-style line endings from manual typing, the cursor offset reported by the framework is incorrect for RTL text in multiline mode on Windows. A commenter linked this to issue #140739 and suspected a connection to CRLF handling. Another commenter noted the bug also manifests with custom fonts on Android (wrong caret position and incorrect word selection). A user shared a workaround that manually inserts `\r\n` instead of `\n` on Enter. The assignee (LongCatIsLooong) was removed after no status updates.

title: Arrow key navigation gets stuck at the end of RTL text using Samsung Keyboard
body:
```dart
const text = 'ی';
final controller = TextEditingController(text: text * 10)..selection = TextSelection.collapsed(offset: 10); // Selection is placed at the logical end of the text.

TextField(textDirection: TextDirection.rtl, controller: controller, autofocus: true),
```
## Steps to reproduce
1. wait for the text field autofocus
2. switch to the Samsung keyboard
3. press <kbd>←</kbd> to move the cursor to the start of the text (since flutter currently uses logical order for arrow key navigation).

## Expected behavior:
the cursor moves to the right

## Actual behavior:
the cursor does not move.
comment_summary: Root cause identified: Samsung keyboard ignores the left-arrow key when the cursor is already at the visual left end of RTL text, because Flutter uses logical order for arrow key navigation while Samsung keyboard uses visual order. Gboard is not affected. On native, arrow key navigation moves in visual order which Flutter does not currently support; a commenter notes this is a long-standing architectural gap.

comment_summary: Reporter was unable to produce a minimal reproducer but identified the trigger: when a Row child with Expanded wrapping a Text widget has its BoxConstraints width collapse to 0, the null assertion fires. The workaround is to wrap both Row children in Expanded. A related PR #155920 was filed but the issue was noted as still needing a fix.

title: Unicode characters not being rendered correctly in goldens test
body:
### Steps to reproduce

When writing a simple goldens test using unicode character \u25CF, it does not render correctly



### Expected results

The character ● displays correctly

### Actual results

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5ed0c550-8437-48ce-9082-e6f39eba9bf8)

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter_test/flutter_test.dart';

class UnicodeCharTestPage extends StatelessWidget {
  const UnicodeCharTestPage({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      body: Column(
        children: [
          TextFormField(
            initialValue: 'abcde',
            obscureText: true,
            obscuringCharacter: '\u25CF',
          ),
        ],
      ),
    );
  }
}

void main() {
  testWidgets('Verify look', (tester) async {
    await tester.pumpWidget(
      const MaterialApp(
        home: UnicodeCharTestPage(),
      ),
    );
    await loadAppFonts();
    await expectLater(
      find.byType(MaterialApp),
      matchesGoldenFile('unicode_char_test.png'),
    );
  });
}
```

</details>

comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue reproduces only in golden tests, not at runtime: the Ahem test font (used by default in matchesGoldenFile) does not include the U+25CF bullet character, rendering it as a tofu box. A commenter suggests switching fonts or using loadAppFonts() properly. Status: open, P3.
